title: allium sphaerocephalon subsp. trachypus
intro: Allium sphaerocephalon subsp. trachypus, also known as the round-headed leek, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the onion family, Amaryllidaceae. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine, where it is valued for its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and heal wounds, as well as reduce swelling and pain. The health benefits of Allium sphaerocephalon subsp. trachypus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce the risk of infection, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
